_meta.note: Living ontology. Grows with the curriculum. Don't overformalize.

LinguisticTarget.description: Atomic language item: a verb form, grammar pattern, or vocabulary unit

fields.lexicalItem: { type, lemma, reflexive? } for verbs, { type, items[] } for vocab groups, { type, pattern } for grammar — reflexive: true means the verb is reflexive (lemma stores the base form, e.g. 'llamar' not 'llamarse')
fields.features: object — mood, tense, person, number, gender, function, etc.
fields.tags: string[] — free-form labels for grouping and content-file bridge

LearningObjective.description: CEFR can-do statement linked to linguistic targets and skills

fields.description: what the student can do after mastering this
fields.gameAction: what the student does in the game
fields.requires: { linguisticTargets: LT IDs[], skills: string[], minEvidence: number, contexts: string[] } — what evidence satisfies this can-do. Formula: evidence[] filtered by (target ∈ linguisticTargets AND skill ∈ skills AND context ∈ contexts).length >= minEvidence → canDoSatisfied

Activity.description: Lean pedagogical unit — what to practice, how, and at what difficulty. Story context comes from the StoryNode at the linked destination.

fields.gameType: narrative | pair | fill | conjugation | listening | category | builder | translation | conversation | dictation | story | cancion | escaperoom | crossword | bingo | scrabble | madlibs | kloo | boggle | spaceman | bananagrams | consequences | madgab | conjuro | explorador | senda | guardian | clon | eco_restaurar | flashnote | cultura | par_minimo | dictogloss | corrector | brecha | resumen | registro | debate | descripcion | ritmo | cronometro | portafolio | autoevaluacion | negociacion | transformador | susurro | eco_lejano | tertulia | pregonero | raiz | codice | sombra | oraculo | tejido | cartografo | despertar
fields.inputMode: choice | typing | voice | drag | listen | self_correction
fields.destination: dest1..dest58 — links Activity to its StoryNode for narrative context
fields.prompt: user-facing instruction tied to story context
fields.targetPerson: optional — narrows a paradigm LT to one person (yo, tú, él/ella/usted, nosotros, ellos/ellas/ustedes)
fields.scaffoldable: boolean — true if adapt_scaffold_input_mode may promote this activity's inputMode. Set on activities with inputMode choice or typing. NOT set on listen, drag, voice, self_correction (mode is inherent to game mechanic or already terminal).
fields.symbolAssist: optional boolean — when true AND destination is A1-A2 Basic, renderers display SYMBOLS SVG icons alongside text. Density auto-calculated from destination number.

EvidenceEvent.description: Runtime record of a learner interacting with an Activity. Written by the engine, read by adaptivity rules. Not authored — generated at runtime.

fields.context: string — story context identifier (destination or narrative moment)

EvidenceEvent.note: EvidenceEvent is runtime-only. It is defined here for documentation and schema validation, but no authored instances exist in the ontology files.

Character.description: A character in the story world

fields.name: display name
fields.role: Campbell archetype (protagonist, mentor, herald, shadow, etc.)
fields.species: jaguar | human | bird | dolphin | turtle | condor | tree | force
fields.relationships: array of { character: ID, relation: string }

Place.description: A specific location in the story world

StoryNode.description: A narrative beat at a specific destination

fields.title: narrative title
fields.closingQuestion: every module ends with a question
fields.preArrival: optional array of despertar encounters shown BEFORE the arrival narrative (A1-A2 Basic only). Each entry: { type: 'despertar', density: 'heavy'|'moderate'|'light', words: [{word, symbol}] }
fields.a1Simplified: optional boolean on arrival narrative — when true, .yg-symbol-word spans get inline SVG icons and TTS click handlers

CulturalConcept.description: A Colombian cultural concept woven into the curriculum

fields.name: display name
fields.category: music | food | tradition | craft | belief | role

MythologicalEntity.description: A mythological or symbolic force in the story

designInvariants._note: Locked rules. Violations break coherence at scale. Do not relax.

rules.rule: Every Activity belongs to a Destination
rules.rationale: No free-floating activities. Destinations are where meaning accumulates. An activity without a destination has no story context, no spiral position, and no CEFR anchor.

The ontology stores lt_verb_gustar_indicative_present, not lt_verb_gustar_indicative_present_yo. Person-narrowing is done via targetPerson on Activity. This prevents LT explosion (6 persons × N verbs × M tenses) and keeps the evidence pipeline clean — evidence accrues to the paradigm, not to one cell.

rules.rule: Scaffolding is global, not authored per-variant
rules.rationale: Activities declare scaffoldable: true. adapt_scaffold_input_mode governs promotion (choice → typing → voice). Do NOT author separate activities for each inputMode of the same meaning. One activity per destination/meaning; the engine adapts.

Rules operate on ontology state (targetHistory, CEFR rank, inputModeLadder), not on specific IDs or strings. Adding a new LinguisticTarget or Activity automatically makes it available to all rules. No rule should contain 'if targetId === lt_verb_...'.

relationships.note: Expressed through ID references in entity fields. No separate graph layer.
